# Scenario

We are interested in exploring the implications of allowing AI, a service generated by the information industry, to replace labor at various costs, for different industries and for different regions.

## Replacing clerical labor with AI

In our first set of scenarios we allow for AI to replace unskilled labor in all industries. We implement this scenario in two stages: first, the AI is introduced in each country alone, then in all developed countries and then finally globally. We consider two different costs for AI implementation, first in a low cost case we assume that one dollar of AI can replace one dollar of labor cost; in the high cost case we assume that two dolars worth of AI are needed to replace one dollar of labor cost.

## Replacing professional staff with AI

In the second set of scenarios, we consider the replacement of technical staff with AI in the manufacturing and service sector. Again, we implement the adotpion of AI in three stages, first by each country alon, then by all developed countries and then, finally, by the entire world. We, again, test two different costs for AI implementation as in the scenarios mentioned above.

## Putting it all together

In the final set of scenarios we analyze the situation where both clerical labor in all industries and technical staff in manufacturing and services is replaced by AI at different costs by developed countries and the entire world.
